_meta.purpose: Tracks all implementation milestones for the Metis Research Cortex redesign. Statuses: pending | in_progress | completed | skipped. Each completed item records a date and optional notes.
phases: Phase 8.8 — Scan for content button (backend ingestion, no Claude Code opens)
Phase 8.9 — Visualization handoff to Claude Design
Phase 8.11 — Learning tab + Course Builder Agent
Phase 8.12 — GitHub README with architecture diagram

items.subject: Create implementation-progress.json and implementation-log.md
items.notes: implementation-progress.json created. implementation-log.md deferred to M0.2 session.

items.subject: Rename 02_agents/pkm-builder/ → 02_agents/rc-builder/ and update all code references
items.notes: Folder renamed (pkm-builder → rc-builder). agent-registry.json updated (slug, name, invocation, chains_with). MCP server.py, agents.py, projects.py docstrings updated.

items.subject: Remove all 'Monia' references from non-venv files
items.notes: Replaced Monia/monia with Metis/metis across code and doc files. History files updated inline. 07_outputs/reviews/monia/ folder deleted (confirmed by user, Red Line 2).

items.subject: Rename 01_control-room/ → 01_journal/, archive planning docs, flatten memory/
items.notes: Planning docs moved to 09_archive/planning-history/. memory/ flattened to direct children. notes/ and brainstorms/ created.

items.subject: Rename 08_system/prompts/ → 08_system/patterns/
items.notes: Renamed via git mv. Aligns with Section 28 Pattern browser decision.

items.subject: Update CLAUDE.md to reflect new folder structure
items.notes: Updated agent invocation table, key paths section, and PKM→RC references.

items.subject: Audit current 12 tabs and decide which code modules will be merged or removed
items.notes: Explore agent audited all 14 R module files. Consolidation plan confirmed: 5 new modules, 3 unchanged, Dropzone removed.

items.subject: Create new 8-tab structure in app.R (Today · Knowledge · Thinking · Planner · Work · Meetings · Learning · Metis) with stub modules
items.notes: app.R updated. 8 nav_panel entries and 8 server calls. All new modules wired.

items.subject: Move Library + Research + News code into new Knowledge module via navset_card_tab
items.notes: mod_knowledge.R created as wrapper. Calls library_ui/server, research_ui/server, news_ui/server as nested sub-modules.

items.subject: Move Notes + Ideas code into new Thinking module via navset_card_tab
items.notes: mod_thinking.R created as wrapper. Calls notes_ui/server (with parent_session) and ideas_ui/server as nested sub-modules.

items.subject: Keep Planner as standalone tab — existing mod_planning.R unchanged
items.notes: No changes needed. planning_ui/planning_server wired directly in app.R.

items.subject: Move Projects code into new Work module
items.notes: mod_work.R created as renamed copy of mod_projects.R. projects_ui → work_ui, projects_server → work_server. h1 heading updated to 'Work'.

items.subject: Keep Meetings as standalone tab — existing mod_meetings.R unchanged
items.notes: No changes needed.

items.subject: Keep Learning as standalone tab — existing mod_learning.R unchanged
items.notes: Learning was already a top-level tab. No changes needed.

items.subject: Move System tab code into new Metis module
items.notes: mod_metis.R created as renamed copy of mod_system.R. system_ui → metis_tab_ui, system_server → metis_tab_server. h1 updated to 'Metis'.

items.subject: Remove Dropzone from nav_panel — not in 8-tab structure
items.notes: Dropzone removed from app.R nav and server calls. mod_dropzone.R remains on disk pending M1.11 cleanup.

items.subject: Delete old replaced modules: mod_control_room.R, mod_projects.R, mod_system.R, mod_dropzone.R
items.notes: User confirmed deletion. All 4 files removed. Sub-modules (library, research, news, notes, ideas) kept — still used by wrapper modules.

items.subject: Visual audit: confirm each tab loads, fits one screen, has clear hierarchy
items.notes: Needs RStudio launch to verify no R errors and 8 tabs render correctly.

items.subject: Strip Today tab to locked layout: greeting strip, overnight summary, launcher row, focus, scan, token footer
items.notes: mod_today.R fully rewritten. Removed KPI boxes, git panel, snapshot, publications panel, project boards, recent files, task table. New structure: 6 sections.

items.subject: Build six launcher cards with Today-level simplified actions
items.notes: launcher-row grid with 6 buttons: Capture idea, Brainstorm, Work on document, Review code, Prep meeting, Process inbox. Each opens a modal with copy-prompt block or navigation action.

items.subject: Build 'What's new overnight' summary
items.notes: overnight-card shows counts for news items, new papers, new ideas, meetings from last 24h. Shows 'clean slate' message when nothing is new.

items.subject: Build 'Today's focus' card
items.notes: focus_ui shows highest-priority active project with domain badge, priority chip, and next step. Conditional — returns NULL when no active projects.

items.subject: Build 'Scan for changes' button and result area
items.notes: Scan button checks: git dirty/unpushed across active projects, tracked_files with watch=1 changed today, PLANNING.md files updated today. Results shown in scan-section groups.

items.subject: Wire trust badge into navbar header
items.notes: nav_spacer() + nav_item(div.trust-badge) added to page_navbar. Shows green shield + 'Local-first' text. Static for now — session call tracking is Phase 4.

items.subject: Build Ctrl+K / Cmd+K shortcut handler + '+ Capture' navbar button
items.notes: JS keydown listener in app.R header fires global_capture input. nav_item button does the same. Both trigger quick_capture_server module via reactive(input$global_capture).

items.subject: Build capture modal with auto-classification (prefix routing)
items.notes: mod_capture.R: quick_capture_server module. Modal shows prefix hints (i: n: t: q:). parse_capture_prefix() classifies text live. renderUI type badge updates as user types. Prefix is stripped before saving.

items.subject: Wire modal to SQLite via capture_idea schema (same DB as MCP tool)
items.notes: i:/default → insert_idea(). n: → insert_quick_note() (personal_notes). t: → insert_task(). q: → insert_idea(idea_type='question'). auto_tags() mirrors MCP tool tag extraction. Also fixed schema bug in mod_today.R quick-capture (was using wrong column names).

items.subject: Design session_events and session_context schemas
Implement session bootstrap stage
Implement Data Guardian intercept stage
Implement Cybersecurity intercept stage
Implement intent parsing stage
Implement token budget allocation stage
Implement surgical context assembly stage
Implement execution with incremental save stage
Implement output red-line post-check
Implement logging stage
Implement self-improvement capture stage
Wire all stages together and run end-to-end pipeline tests
Install sqlite-vec and set up vector tables
items.notes: sqlite-vec 0.1.9 installed in venv. vec_episodic, vec_semantic, vec_procedural virtual tables defined in vector_memory.py DDL.

items.subject: Install sentence-transformers and pick embedding model
items.notes: Using fastembed 0.8.0 (ONNX, no PyTorch needed) instead of sentence-transformers. Model: BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5, 384 dims. embeddings.py provides lazy-loaded embed()/embed_one(). Model downloads to ~/.cache/fastembed on first use.

items.subject: Implement episodic memory layer
items.notes: store_episodic_memory() MCP tool. Table: episodic_memory + vec_episodic virtual table. Stores events with type (idea/note/task/paper/meeting/agent_run), content, metadata, session_id.

items.subject: Implement semantic memory graph
items.notes: store_semantic_memory() MCP tool. Table: semantic_memory + vec_semantic. Stores concept/definition nodes with related_concepts and source linkage.

items.subject: Implement procedural memory tracker
items.notes: store_procedural_memory() MCP tool. Table: procedural_memory + vec_procedural. Stores workflow patterns with trigger_context and step-by-step instructions.

items.subject: Implement working memory
items.notes: set_working_memory() + get_working_memory() MCP tools. Table: working_memory. Per-session key/value scratchpad for mid-pipeline state.

items.subject: Implement reflexive memory
items.notes: Handled by write_reflexion() in self_improvement.py (Phase 4). reflexion_log table captures agent self-critiques. No separate implementation needed.

items.subject: Build retrieval pipeline with RRF fusion and reranking
items.notes: semantic_search() MCP tool. Vector similarity (sqlite-vec KNN) + keyword LIKE fused via RRF (k=60). Searches episodic, semantic, and procedural layers. Returns top_k ranked results.

items.subject: Build retrieval debugger UI in Metis → Memory
items.notes: Needs R Shiny tab in mod_metis.R to call semantic_search() and display layered results. Defer to after MCP tools are confirmed working.

items.subject: anonymize_text MCP tool — regex PII scrubbing
items.notes: tools/anonymization.py: anonymize_text(content, mode, replace_names). Reuses safety.py patterns: PATIENT_ID, GPS, Belgian NID, EMAIL, PHONE. Optional name heuristic (CAPITALIZED tokens). Returns JSON with anonymized text + replacements map + counters.

items.subject: diff_anonymization MCP tool — unified diff view
items.notes: diff_anonymization(original, anonymized): Python difflib unified_diff. Returns plain text diff suitable for display in dashboard or agent output.

items.subject: Trust badge enhanced with network policy indicator
items.notes: main.py trust_badge(): reads system/config/network-policy.json. Icons: bi-shield-lock (strict/warning), bi-wifi-off (offline/danger), bi-shield-check (normal). Badge updates every 60s.

items.subject: Consent ledger MCP tools + dashboard card
items.notes: log_consent_event() + get_consent_ledger() MCP tools. consent_ledger table (id, timestamp, action, data_classification, agent_slug, notes, session_id). Metis tab card shows recent events with colour-coded classification badges.

items.subject: Network policy MCP tools + dashboard badge
items.notes: set_network_policy(policy) + get_network_policy() MCP tools. Persists to system/config/network-policy.json. Policies: strict/normal/offline. Dashboard consent card header shows live badge via /api/partial/metis/network-policy.

items.subject: backup_db MCP tool — SQLite Online Backup API
items.notes: tools/backup.py: backup_db(destination, label, extra_destination). Uses sqlite3.Connection.backup() — safe for live WAL databases. Produces timestamped .sqlite files. Returns path, size, SHA-256 checksum, elapsed_ms.

items.subject: encrypt_backup MCP tool — AES-256-GCM
items.notes: encrypt_backup(backup_path, passphrase). Uses cryptography 46.0.6 (PBKDF2-SHA256, 200k rounds, AES-256-GCM). Falls back to XOR-SHA256 with warning if library absent. Format: METISBK1 magic + 16B salt + 12B nonce + ciphertext+tag.

items.subject: list_backups + multi-destination write
items.notes: list_backups(backup_dir): scans .sqlite/.enc files, returns name/path/size/age/type. backup_db() supports extra_destination for off-site copy.

items.subject: restore_db MCP tool — safe restore with pre-restore copy
items.notes: restore_db(backup_path, confirm='YES'). Renames live DB to .pre-restore.<ts>.sqlite before overwriting. Returns status, paths, and restart reminder.

items.subject: verify_backup MCP tool — PRAGMA integrity_check
items.notes: verify_backup(backup_path): opens backup in read-only mode, runs PRAGMA integrity_check, returns status/tables/messages. Also: set_backup_schedule + get_backup_schedule persist nightly schedule to system/config/backup-schedule.json for Phase 10 scheduler.

items.subject: Test full wipe-and-restore cycle
items.notes: Manual verification — run backup_db, verify_backup, restore_db in sequence against a copy of the live DB.

items.subject: brainstorm_turn MCP tool + session table
items.notes: tools/brainstorm.py: brainstorm_turn(topic, steering, session_uuid, turn_notes). Fetches context: ideas, personal_notes, open questions, library .md notes. 5 steering modes (expand/focus/challenge/synthesize/connect) each with 3 steering prompts. brainstorm_sessions + brainstorm_turns SQLite tables.

items.subject: Plan Mode system prompt + patterns file
items.notes: system/config/patterns/brainstorm-plan-mode.md: protocol (call brainstorm_turn, generate 3-5 insights, offer 3 steering questions per turn), tone rules, anti-patterns. Activated by /metis_brainstorm skill.

items.subject: Steering buttons UI in Thinking tab
items.notes: Thinking tab brainstorm panel: topic input, 5 steering buttons (Expand/Focus/Challenge/Synthesize/Connect), 'Copy prompt for Claude Code' button that generates /metis_brainstorm prompt with selected steering. Recent sessions list via /api/partial/thinking/brainstorm-sessions.

items.subject: Cross-pollinate session context
items.notes: get_brainstorm_session(session_uuid) + list_brainstorm_sessions(limit) MCP tools provide session history for cross-session cross-pollination. Context payload returned by brainstorm_turn() already surfaces cross-domain library connections.

items.subject: Structured output format for saved brainstorms
items.notes: save_brainstorm_output(session_uuid, title, synthesis, action_items): writes outputs/brainstorms/<date>_<slug>.md with synthesis, action items, and turn log. Updates brainstorm_sessions.status to 'saved'.

items.subject: profile_dataset MCP tool
items.notes: tools/data_tools.py: profile_dataset(path, sample_rows). Shape, dtypes, null %, unique counts, numeric distributions (min/max/mean/median/p25/p75), top-5 values for categoricals. PII column scan before profiling. Supports CSV, TSV, Excel, SPSS (.sav), Stata (.dta).

items.subject: suggest_cleaning MCP tool
items.notes: suggest_cleaning(path): profiles the dataset and returns prioritised cleaning recommendations with rationale (high/medium/low). Checks duplicates, null %, whitespace, date columns.

items.subject: clean_dataset MCP tool
items.notes: clean_dataset(path, operations, output_path): applies drop_duplicates, drop_columns, fill_na, rename_column, strip_whitespace, standardize_dates, drop_na_rows, drop_na_rows_any. Never modifies original. Returns row/col delta.

items.subject: compare_profiles + list_supported_formats MCP tools
items.notes: compare_profiles(before_json, after_json): side-by-side diff with human_summary. list_supported_formats(): returns supported extensions + library versions.

items.subject: data-analyst agent (system-prompt + contract + registry)
items.notes: agents/data-analyst/system-prompt.md: 7-step workflow (intake → before report → propose → approve → clean → after report → audit). agents/data-analyst/contract.md. agent-registry.json entry (write-local tier). CLAUDE.md routing + invocation table updated. Packages installed: pandas 3.0.2, openpyxl 3.1.5, pyreadstat 1.3.4.

Phase 8.6.label: CLI Command Layer
Phase 8.6.notes: Skills that mirror dashboard tabs from the Claude Code CLI. Naming convention: all non-agent commands start with /metis_. Agents keep their own slash command.

items.subject: /metis_info — full command reference for Claude Code power users
items.notes: metis/.claude/skills/metis-info/skill.md. Static output: all commands grouped by category (daily workflow, agents, setup). Haiku, quick. No MCP calls — instant response.

items.subject: /metis_morning — daily morning briefing (Today tab equivalent in CLI)
items.notes: metis/.claude/skills/metis-morning/skill.md. Pulls: news_briefs, tasks (open/in_progress/blocked), project_status, agent_runs (overnight), PLANNING.md files. Output: focus block, top tasks, PhD pulse, overnight, news signals. Sonnet, standard. Saves to outputs/reviews/metis/.

items.subject: /metis_projects — active projects overview (Work/Planner tabs equivalent)
items.notes: metis/.claude/skills/metis-projects/skill.md. Reads project_status + tasks + PLANNING.md for each active project. Status indicators: active/stalled/blocked/someday. Single-project deep-dive mode via /metis_projects [name]. Haiku, quick.

items.subject: /metis_agents — agent directory with last-run dates (Metis tab equivalent)
items.notes: metis/.claude/skills/metis-agents/skill.md. Lists all 18 agents grouped by domain with capability descriptions, when-to-use guidance, and last-run date from agent_runs. Single-agent deep-dive reads system-prompt.md. Haiku, quick.

items.subject: /metis_tasks [filter] — task list with filters (Work tab equivalent)
items.notes: metis-tasks skill.md. Haiku. Quick. Accepts optional filter: blocked/overdue/[project-slug]/all.

items.subject: /metis_phd — PhD article status and next milestone (Planner PhD board equivalent)
items.notes: metis-phd skill.md. Sonnet. Standard. Reads phd-framework PLANNING.md + domain notes. Deep-dive per article.

items.subject: /metis_literature [query] — library search and Zotero status (Knowledge tab equivalent)
items.notes: metis-literature skill.md. Haiku. Quick. Accepts optional search query. Shows library stats + recent additions.

items.subject: /metis_capture [text] — quick capture from terminal (capture modal equivalent)
items.notes: metis-capture skill.md. Haiku. Quick. Prefix routing: i: idea, n: note, t: task, q: question.

items.subject: /metis_inbox — scan inbox/ folder and route items to agents
items.notes: metis-inbox skill.md. Sonnet. Standard. Scans inbox/ folder + inbox tasks. Proposes routing, waits for user confirm.

items.subject: /metis_news — curated news briefs (Today news section equivalent)
items.notes: metis-news skill.md. Haiku. Quick. Accepts optional domain filter (HAT/AI/public-health/methods). Groups by domain.

Phase 8.7.label: Editorial redesign & full functionality
Phase 8.7.notes: Matches Claude Design's Metis_Design.png proposal. Research Desk / editorial newspaper layout. Fixes the functional gap where launcher cards and many tabs were read-only. Reference: /home/sverschaeve/.claude/plans/nifty-stargazing-sifakis.md has the full visual spec for continued Claude Design work.

items.subject: CSS v7.0/v7.1 — warm editorial tokens + editorial layout classes
items.notes: styles.css v7.1. Single coherent palette (dropped dual macOS/warm split). Added .dateline-strip, .today-hero (grid with stats right-aligned), .today-canvas (3-col: focus|activity|news), .focus-thread (serif, pull-quote), .activity-feed (mono time + sans content), .today-news-rail (category chips with count + age), .question-prompt (Georgia italic input, no border, teal send circle), .today-actions-strip, .metis-toast, .news-modal-overlay. Larger type scale: greeting 3.1rem, narrative 2.05rem italic, focus-title 1.75rem, focus-quote 1.15rem italic with teal left rule.

items.subject: today.html restructured — dateline → hero → 3-col canvas → actions → question
items.notes: Replaced the dashboard-grid layout with editorial structure. Each section loads via HTMX. Question prompt at bottom uses italic serif placeholder 'What are you thinking about?' with mode toggles (idea/task/note/ask metis).

items.subject: Today partials — dateline, hero, focus-thread, activity-feed, news-rail, news-modal
items.notes: 6 new partials in templates/partials/: today_dateline.html (with Last scan + Scan now), today_hero.html (greeting + 3 ambient stats), today_focus_thread.html (resumed/quote/scan sections), today_activity_feed.html (news/agent/meeting/idea rows, clickable), today_news_rail.html (category chips + items), news_summary_modal.html. Clickable news titles route through openNewsSummary().

items.subject: Today router — new endpoints for dateline/hero/focus/activity/news-rail/news-modal
items.notes: routers/today.py: /api/partial/today/dateline, /api/partial/today/hero, /api/partial/today/focus-thread, /api/partial/today/activity-feed, /api/partial/today/news-rail (accepts ?category=X filter), /api/news/brief/{id}. Hero narrative is dynamic: 'N threads need you today.' based on open tasks. Focus thread reads active project + latest idea/note as pull-quote. Activity unions news/agent_runs/meetings/ideas from last 48h.

items.subject: openCapture(prefillMode) signature fixed + all callers
items.notes: app.js v7.1. Fixes the bug where work.html:13 and thinking.html:13,48,72 were calling openCapture('task'/'idea'/etc) against a zero-arg definition. Now openCapture('idea') prefills the textarea with 'i: '.

items.subject: Launcher cards actually open programs (not just copy prompts)
items.notes: 5 cards wired to launchPrompt() which POSTs to /api/project/launch with target+prompt. Config: brainstorm→claude_code+/metis_brainstorm, write→claude_code+/writing-partner, review→vscode+followUp claude_code, meeting→claude_code+/meeting-memory, inbox→claude_code+/metis_inbox. Prompt also copied to clipboard as safety net. 'Capture idea' still opens local modal.

items.subject: News summary modal + task action buttons
items.notes: Click any news item (in activity feed OR news rail) → modal opens via /api/news/brief/{id} with title, domain, signal, summary, source link. Task rows in work.html now show done (✓) + delete (🗑) buttons on hover → POST /api/task/{id}/done|delete.

items.subject: Project launcher endpoint — port R/Shiny logic to FastAPI
items.notes: routers/work.py: POST /api/project/launch accepts project_id, target (rstudio|vscode|explorer|claude_desktop|claude_code), and optional prompt. Reuses logic from legacy system/app/R/mod_work.R (lines 29-160). WSL→Windows path conversion via _wsl_to_windows(). Runs via /mnt/c/Windows/System32/cmd.exe. Special project_id 'rc-root' uses METIS_RC_ROOT. Only launches projects that exist in DB — prevents arbitrary execution.

items.subject: Seed launcher_type for 5 active projects
items.notes: hat-dashboard=rstudio, hat-clustering=rstudio, multilevel-analysis=vscode, phd-framework=vscode, passive-screening-drc=rstudio. external_path already set for 3 (hat-dashboard, hat-clustering, multilevel-analysis). STILL PENDING: phd-framework + passive-screening-drc need external_path values from user.

items.subject: Per-project 'Open in...' dropdown on work_projects.html
items.notes: Each active project card shows buttons: RStudio (conditional on launcher_type), VS Code, Claude Code (Windows Terminal + claude), Claude Chat (Claude Desktop via protocol), Explorer, GitHub (if github_url set). All call launchProjectTarget(id, target) → POST /api/project/launch.

items.subject: Scan trigger on dateline — refresh HTMX partials
items.notes: Dateline now shows 'Last scan · Nh ago · [Scan now]'. runDashboardScan() in app.js re-triggers the load events on all today partials. Gives the 'I was working elsewhere, catch me up' workflow a single-click entry.

items.subject: Seed external_path for phd-framework and passive-screening-drc
items.notes: Needs confirmation from user of canonical Windows paths for these two projects. Without it, their 'Open in...' buttons show 'No external path configured'.

items.subject: End-to-end test from Windows browser — actually open VS Code/Claude Code/RStudio
items.notes: Manual click-through test by user

items.subject: Decide whether 'Review code' card should chain VS Code + Claude Code
items.notes: Current behavior: opens VS Code in focus project, then 500ms later also opens Claude Code. Two windows might be overkill — consider making this a single choice (VS Code only OR Claude Code only) based on user preference.

items.subject: Refine news rail categories + add 'last updated per category' affordance
items.notes: Current rail shows domain chips with age. User may want a different taxonomy (HAT / AI / Public Health / Research / Methods / Geopolitics) or a different display per category. Needs ~10 real news items across domains to evaluate the pattern (currently only 4 rows). AUTOMATIC ONCE M8.8.x SHIPS — scan will populate enough items to evaluate.

Phase 8.8.label: Scan for content button (backend ingestion, no Claude Code opens)
Phase 8.8.notes: Full spec in metis/journal/2026-04-24_sonnet_handoff_plan.md §2. Primary user ask: one dashboard button that triggers news-radar-like + librarian-like ingestion server-side, no external apps open.

items.subject: Create content_scan.py in MCP server (feedparser + literature folder scan)
Register scan_news_feeds + scan_literature_folder as MCP tools
items.notes: MCP tool registration deferred — functions usable directly from FastAPI without MCP wrapping

items.subject: Add POST /api/scan/content endpoint in routers/today.py
Add 'Scan for content' button to dateline partial + runContentScan() in app.js
Install feedparser in MCP venv + update requirements.txt
Verify end-to-end: click button → feeds fetched → new rows in news_briefs → news rail refreshes
items.notes: Requires dashboard restart to test end-to-end

items.subject: Close out Phase 8.7 quick wins: simplify Review-code launcher (VS Code only), ask user for 2 external_paths

Phase 8.9.label: Visualization handoff to Claude Design
Phase 8.9.notes: Minimal handoff file so user's Claude Design tokens are spent on design, not re-briefing.

items.subject: Create metis/system/design-docs/2026-04-24_claude_design_handoff.md with pillars list
Create metis/system/design-docs/deliveries/.gitkeep for future design iterations
Tell user how to hand off: paste handoff file as first message in Claude Design project; pick ONE pillar per session
items.notes: Handoff file ready at metis/system/design-docs/2026-04-24_claude_design_handoff.md. User pastes into Claude Design project.

Phase 8.10.label: Resume post-Claude-Design integration
Phase 8.10.notes: Auto-trigger on user saying 'Continue what you were doing' or 'Resume post-Claude-Design'. See §4 of handoff plan.

items.subject: Write detection protocol: scan design-docs/deliveries/ for newest folder, read CHANGES.md
Integrate delivery: copy CSS/partials, bump styles.css version, verify endpoints still 200

Phase 8.11.label: Learning tab + Course Builder Agent
Phase 8.11.notes: Full spec in handoff plan §5. Rename MLM → statistics-full/statistics-adaptive. Course Builder orchestrates Content Harvester + Learning Architect + domain expert + Writing Partner + Visualization Maker. 15 placeholder courses seeded.

items.subject: Rename MLM project to statistics-full in DB + CLAUDE.md
items.notes: Updated learning_courses id=6: slug=statistics-full, title=Statistics for Epidemiology (full). projects.multilevel-analysis unchanged (teaching project).

items.subject: Create learning_courses table (DDL in handoff plan §5.2.2)
items.notes: learning_courses table already existed. Added slug TEXT column via ALTER TABLE. Existing schema: id, title, category, progress_pct, total_modules, completed_modules, status, completed_at, created_at, slug.

items.subject: Seed 15 placeholder courses
items.notes: Seeded 14 placeholder courses (ids 7-20). statistics-full already exists as id=6. All inserted with status=placeholder.

items.subject: Write course-builder-questionnaire.md (7-section intake)
items.notes: Created metis/system/config/course-builder-questionnaire.md — 7 sections: audience, depth, subject matter, delivery, style, review, updating.

items.subject: Create Course Builder agent (system-prompt.md, contract.md, registry entry, CLAUDE.md routing)
items.notes: Created metis/agents/course-builder/system-prompt.md (7-step workflow), contract.md. Added to agent-registry.json. Added to CLAUDE.md invocation table + routing table.

items.subject: Add 'Build this course' button + buildCourse() JS + /api/course/build-request endpoint
items.notes: Added Build button to learning_placeholder_courses.html and Continue building to learning_courses.html. Added buildCourse() to app.js. Added /api/course/build-request endpoint + /api/partial/learning/placeholder-courses endpoint in routers/learning.py.

items.subject: Import MLM reference files (IMPLEMENTATION-PLAN.md, PLANNING.md, app patterns) to _reference folder
items.notes: MLM reference file import deferred — the teaching project files are at external_path in projects table. Course Builder can import via /course-builder import {path} at build time.

items.subject: Course open-in-new-tab decision: add /course/{slug} route stub for future course pages
items.notes: Course open-in-new-tab stub deferred to when first course is actually built.

Phase 8.12.label: GitHub README with architecture diagram
Phase 8.12.notes: Content ready in handoff plan §6.3. Mermaid flowchart shows user → Metis router → specialist agents, with Data Guardian + Cybersecurity watching + self-improvement loop feedback.

items.subject: Write README.md at repo root (content in handoff plan §6.3)
items.notes: README.md at repo root with Mermaid architecture diagram, features grid, 3 workflows, getting started block.

items.subject: Verify Mermaid renders correctly via GitHub preview
items.notes: User needs to push and verify Mermaid renders on GitHub.

items.subject: Create docs/screenshots/.gitkeep for future visual assets
items.notes: docs/screenshots/.gitkeep created.

Phase 8.13.label: Token efficiency + auto-handoff loop
Phase 8.13.notes: Full spec in handoff plan §7. Three additions: token pulse widget, auto-handoff at 80% turns, implementation-plan-change detector on resume.

items.subject: Add /api/partial/metis/token-pulse endpoint + hero widget showing rolling 24h tokens

items.subject: CSS animations & polish (v8.1)

items.subject: Button wiring across all 9 tabs

Teach (History/Continue/Publish via metisStub copy-prompt; Start building / View catalog). Metis (Rename via /api/identity/rename; Keys hint; Export hint; model USE via /api/model/active). Planner (Retire/Pause/Schedule via /api/task/oldest-open/{action}). Tune scan modal.

items.subject: Backend endpoints for new actions
items.notes: Added: POST /api/note/from-latest-idea (thinking.py), POST /api/task/oldest-open/{action} (work.py), POST /api/model/active (metis_tab.py), POST /api/identity/rename (metis_tab.py). User prefs persisted to system/config/user-preferences.json.

items.subject: FastAPI scaffold — system/app-py/, deps, base layout + nav
items.notes: main.py, db.py, launch.py, run.sh, requirements.txt. 9 routers. Bootstrap 5 + HTMX navbar with 8 tabs, trust badge, capture button. CSS copied from R app unchanged.

items.subject: DB layer — port db_table(), metis_paths(), schema helpers to Python
items.notes: db.py: get_db_path() (METIS_RC_ROOT env var aware), db_query(), db_scalar(), db_execute(). Graceful fallbacks for missing tables.

items.subject: Today tab — daily summary, trust badge, session count
items.notes: Greeting strip, 6 launcher cards, overnight summary, today's focus, scan for changes (git status), token footer. All HTMX partials.

items.subject: Knowledge tab — cards, search, add/edit
items.notes: Library cards, literature table, domain breakdown, live search via HTMX. Stats row. Partial templates: knowledge_stats, knowledge_library, knowledge_literature, knowledge_domains, knowledge_search.

items.subject: Meetings + Learning + Work tabs — tables, details, progress
items.notes: Meetings: filterable table, search, stats. Learning: spaced-repetition due-today, course progress bars, completed list, competency map. Work: stats, filterable task list with priority icons, active project cards.

items.subject: Thinking tab — notes, connections
items.notes: Ideas list, personal notes, open questions panel, brainstorm launcher card. Capture links (Ctrl+K → i:/n:/q: prefixes).

items.subject: Planner tab — kanban, focus board, timeline
items.notes: 3-view toggle: kanban (someday/incubating/active columns), PhD focus board (projects + tasks filtered by domain), timeline table with start/target dates.

items.subject: Metis tab — agent runs, config panel
items.notes: Stats row (runs today, tokens, total, active agents), filterable run history table, agent registry from agent-registry.json, system info (RC root, DB path + size).

items.subject: Teach tab — 9th tab for classes the researcher teaches
items.notes: Course cards with Librarian lit alerts (new papers last 30d), News Aggregator alerts (signals last 7d), Chat/Co-work/Slides/Assessment/Q-bank/Gap-analysis action buttons per course. Add-course modal. routers/teach.py, templates/teach.html, 3 partial templates.

items.subject: Quick-capture — Ctrl+K modal, capture endpoint
items.notes: routers/capture.py: GET /api/capture-modal, POST /api/capture with i:/n:/t:/q: prefix routing. app.js: Ctrl+K shortcut, live type badge.

items.subject: DB watcher — poll for MCP updates, reload notification
items.notes: GET /api/check-db-mtime endpoint. app.js polls every 20s, shows Bootstrap alert with 'Reload now' link when mtime changes.

items.notes: agents/design-auditor/: 7 scored dimensions, anti-pattern library with severity ratings, 9-step workflow.

items.subject: Update CLAUDE.md + routing table — new agent names, invocations
items.notes: CLAUDE.md updated: frontend-designer, design-auditor, visualization-maker, content-harvester, learning-architect added. Routing table updated.

items.subject: Add gstack workflow patterns to relevant agents
items.notes: office-hours, design-shotgun, qa patterns for Frontend Designer Builder and Software Engineer.

items.subject: Update implementation-progress.json — add Phase 5.4, 5.5, 5.6 milestones
items.notes: This update.

items.subject: Injection probe middleware for external tool results
items.notes: tools/guardrails.py: injection_probe(content) + probe_tool_result MCP tool. Detects 11 adversarial patterns + zero-width chars. Prepends INJECTION WARNING prefix (non-blocking — annotates, doesn't block). Primary surface: content-harvester, news-aggregator, librarian ingestion.

items.subject: max_turns enforcement with graceful truncation
items.notes: pipeline.py run_metis(): new max_turns=20 parameter. Counts session_events WHERE event_type='turn' for current session. Returns {status: truncated} message when exceeded, directing user to start a new session.

items.subject: Constitutional policy file + loader
items.notes: system/config/constitution.md: 12 rules across Clinical Safety, Statistical Integrity, Data Protection, Agent Behaviour, Routing, Research Integrity, PhD Protection. tools/guardrails.py: load_constitution(level) loads CRITICAL rules for quick/standard, all rules for deep/chain. Pipeline Stage 7.5 calls it automatically. get_constitution MCP tool exposes it.

items.subject: Multi-agent trust validation
items.notes: tools/guardrails.py: validate_agent_output(output, required_keys) + validate_pipeline_stage MCP tool. Treats sub-agent output with same suspicion as external tool output. Validates required keys before passing to next pipeline stage.

items.subject: YAML frontmatter on all 31 knowledge/library/ .md files
items.notes: YAML header added to 31 files: domain, tags (4-8 terms), related (2-7 links), phd_relevance (high/medium/low), status, updated. Defines graph edges via 'related' list.

items.subject: note_links SQLite table + DDL migration
items.notes: note_links table: source_path, target_path, link_type, source_title, target_title. Unique index on (source_path, target_path). Created on first kg_index_notes() run. 95 bidirectional pairs = 190 directed edges.

items.subject: MCP tools: kg_index_notes, kg_paths, kg_community
items.notes: tools/knowledge_graph.py: kg_index_notes() scans frontmatter → populates note_links; kg_paths(from, to, max_hops) BFS shortest paths; kg_community(note, depth) connected cluster. Server now has 74 tools.

items.subject: Graph view in Knowledge tab — D3 force-directed graph
items.notes: 4th nav pill 'Graph' in Knowledge tab. D3 v7 force-directed graph: nodes colored by domain, sized by phd_relevance, click-to-detail panel, filter input, zoom/pan, domain legend. Data API at /api/knowledge/graph-data.

items.subject: agent_spans table + observability DDL
items.notes: tools/observability.py: agent_spans table (span_id TEXT PK, parent_id, run_id, session_id, name, kind, status, start_ms, end_ms, duration_ms, error, tags, created_at). WAL-mode compatible.

items.subject: MCP tools: start_span, end_span, log_span, get_spans
items.notes: start_span() returns UUID span_id; end_span(span_id) calculates duration_ms from start_ms; log_span() one-shot for completed spans; get_spans() filtered by session/run. Server now has 78 tools.

items.subject: Span waterfall in Metis tab
items.notes: Traces card in metis_tab.html. /api/partial/metis/traces route in metis_tab.py. CSS horizontal waterfall bars sized by duration_ms, colored by kind (internal/tool/agent/llm). Run header shows status badge + timestamp. Legend rendered when >1 kind used.
